reason: Svelte 4→5 migrate fixture using lang=scss CSS; rsvelte correctly rejects the SCSS $-variable syntax (css_expected_identifier)
Svelte 4→5 migrate fixture using lang=scss CSS; rsvelte correctly rejects the SCSS $-variable syntax (css_expected_identifier)
Svelte 4→5 migrate output using legacy let: directives and slot= attributes; Svelte 4 migrator is out of scope per AGENTS.md
Svelte 4→5 migrate output; Svelte 4 migrator is out of scope per AGENTS.md
oxfmt/prettier-plugin-svelte silently drops nested rest destructuring variables: {#each array as [first, second, ...[third, ...{length}]]} becomes ...[...undefined], losing 'third' and 'length'
oxfmt/prettier-plugin-svelte silently drops nested rest destructuring: {:then [a, b, ...[, c, ...{length}]]} becomes ...[...undefined], losing all inner variables
oxfmt/prettier-plugin-svelte silently drops nested rest destructuring: {#each a as [first, second, ...[third, ...{length}]]} becomes ...[...undefined]
oxfmt drops the closing paren in {@const x = (h = 0)}, producing {@const x = (h = 0} — invalid Svelte syntax
oxfmt collapses whitespace-significant <textarea> content (multiline 'A\nB' → ' A B '), destroying the whitespace that the runtime relies on
oxfmt collapses <textarea> child whitespace onto one line, losing the original newlines/spaces in whitespace-sensitive content
oxfmt emits a tab-indented CSS selector (\th1 {) after a spaces-indented comment block, mixing tabs and spaces in one rule set
oxfmt mixes indentation: .foo, is spaces-indented but the following .bar selector is tab-indented (\t.bar), producing inconsistent whitespace
oxfmt emits tab-only indentation for :is() inner selectors (\t\tbutton) while outer rules use spaces (2-space indent), mixing tab and space indentation in one stylesheet

prettier-plugin-svelte parses <script     



> (whitespace inside close tag) as an empty script and loses the body content (let name = 'world'); oracle emits <script></script> with no body

prettier-plugin-svelte parses <style     



> (whitespace inside close tag) as an empty style and loses the body (div { color: red; }); oracle emits <style></style> with no body
Snippet param 'c?: number = 5' is invalid TypeScript (TS1015: parameter cannot have both ? and an initializer); rsvelte-fmt correctly rejects it (script parse error from oxc)
Snippets do not support rest parameters (snippet_invalid_rest_parameter); rsvelte-fmt correctly rejects this invalid input; oracle (prettier-plugin-svelte) formats it anyway without validation

oxfmt collapses whitespace-significant <textarea> content (multi-line garbage + split close-tags) onto one line, destroying whitespace the textarea would render; same class as textarea-content/textarea-value-children. rsvelte preserves the content.

oxc vs prettier ternary line-break granularity: in a long class= the oracle breaks the ternary CONDITION at === while oxc either keeps === together or breaks every nested condition. JS-engine choice (rsvelte uses oxc by design); not width-fixable. Upstream oxc-alignment item.

oxc vs prettier template-literal ${} substitution indentation inside <script> (ternary indented 8/10 spaces by the oracle vs 4/6 by oxc). JS-engine formatting of the script body; rsvelte delegates to oxc_formatter. Upstream oxc-alignment item.

oxc vs prettier break-priority in a {#if} block header: the oracle breaks ONLY the inner member chain (contents
.trim()
.toLowerCase()) keeping `unique && value.some(...)` on line 1; oxc_formatter breaks the && / call-args instead and cannot produce the oracle chain-only break at any width. JS-engine choice, not an rsvelte layout bug. Upstream oxc-alignment item.

oxfmt produces PLATFORM-DEPENDENT output for this file: on macOS it collapses the overflowing self-closing <ColorIndicator color={value} /> inside <pre> to one line, on Linux it attribute-wraps it. The oracle is thus self-inconsistent across OSes, so byte-parity is undefined (a deterministic formatter cannot match both). Upstream oxfmt cross-platform non-determinism bug. rsvelte deterministically matches the macOS form.
